---
title: "PUMS Data EDA - Variable Selection with LASSO"
output: html_notebook
---

## Load packages and data

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(glmnet)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

# Read household-level variables
households <- read_csv(
  file = file.path("data", "psam_h06.csv"),
  col_select = c(
    "SERIALNO",
    "ACCESSINET",  # Internet access
    "TEL",         # Telephone service
    "TEN",         # Tenure (own/rent)
    "VEH",         # Vehicles available
    "BDSP",        # Bedrooms
    "VALP",        # Property value
    "GRPIP"        # Gross rent as % of income
  )
)

# Read person-level variables
persons <- read_csv(
  file = file.path("data", "psam_p06.csv"),
  col_select = c(
    "SERIALNO",
    "PWGTP",       # Person weight
    "PUMA",        # Geographic area
    # Demographics
    "AGEP",        # Age
    "SEX",         # Sex
    "RAC1P",       # Race
    "HISP",        # Hispanic origin
    "MAR",         # Marital status
    "CIT",         # Citizenship
    "NATIVITY",    # Native/foreign born
    "LANX",        # Language other than English
    # Economic
    "WAGP",        # Wages/salary
    "ESR",         # Employment status
    "WKHP",        # Hours worked per week
    "COW",         # Class of worker
    # Education
    "SCHL",        # Educational attainment
    # Health
    "DIS",         # Disability status
    # Transportation (for JWMNP model)
    "JWMNP",       # Travel time to work
    "JWTRNS",      # Means of transportation
    # Response variables
    "POVPIP",      # Income-to-poverty ratio
    "HICOV"        # Health insurance
  )
)

# Join person and household data
acs_pop <- persons %>% left_join(households, by = "SERIALNO")

# Exclude group quarters (only households)
acs_pop <- acs_pop %>%
  filter(substr(SERIALNO, start = 5, stop = 6) == "HU")

cat("Total records:", nrow(acs_pop), "\n")
cat("Number of PUMAs:", n_distinct(acs_pop$PUMA), "\n")
```

## Examine Missing Values in Individual-Level Data

```{r missing_values}
# Calculate missingness for each variable
missing_summary <- acs_pop %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(
    n_total = nrow(acs_pop),
    pct_missing = (n_missing / n_total) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_missing))

cat("\n=== MISSING VALUES SUMMARY ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Total records: %d\n\n", nrow(acs_pop)))

# Show all variables with their missingness
cat("All variables (sorted by % missing):\n\n")
print(missing_summary, n = Inf)

# Flag variables with high missingness
cat("\n=== VARIABLES WITH >10% MISSING ===\n")
high_missing <- missing_summary %>% filter(pct_missing > 10)
print(high_missing, n = Inf)
```

## Process and create PUMA-level aggregates

```{r aggregate}
# Create individual-level processed variables
acs_processed <- acs_pop %>%
  mutate(
    # Binary indicators for aggregation
    has_hicov = ifelse(HICOV == 1, 1, 0),
    in_poverty = ifelse(POVPIP < 100, 1, 0),  # Below poverty line
    is_male = ifelse(SEX == 1, 1, 0),
    is_white = ifelse(RAC1P == 1, 1, 0),
    is_black = ifelse(RAC1P == 2, 1, 0),
    is_asian = ifelse(RAC1P == 6, 1, 0),
    is_hispanic = ifelse(HISP != "01", 1, 0),
    is_married = ifelse(MAR == 1, 1, 0),
    is_citizen = ifelse(CIT %in% c(1, 2, 3, 4), 1, 0),
    is_foreign_born = ifelse(NATIVITY == 2, 1, 0),
    speaks_other_lang = ifelse(LANX == 1, 1, 0),
    is_employed = ifelse(ESR == 1, 1, 0),
    has_bachelor = ifelse(SCHL >= 21, 1, 0),  # Bachelor's or higher
    has_disability = ifelse(DIS == 1, 1, 0),
    drives_alone = ifelse(JWTRNS == 1, 1, 0),
    uses_transit = ifelse(JWTRNS %in% c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6), 1, 0),
    is_homeowner = ifelse(TEN %in% c(1, 2), 1, 0),
    has_internet = ifelse(ACCESSINET %in% c(1, 2, 3), 1, 0),
    has_phone = ifelse(TEL == 1, 1, 0)
  )

# Aggregate to PUMA level
puma_data <- acs_processed %>%
  group_by(PUMA) %>%
  summarise(
    # Sample size
    n = n(),

    # Response variables (2 outcomes)
    hicov_rate = mean(has_hicov, na.rm = TRUE),
    poverty_rate = mean(in_poverty, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Predictor: mean commute time
    mean_commute = mean(JWMNP, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Demographics
    mean_age = mean(AGEP, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_age = sd(AGEP, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_male = mean(is_male, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_white = mean(is_white, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_black = mean(is_black, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_asian = mean(is_asian, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_hispanic = mean(is_hispanic, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_married = mean(is_married, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_citizen = mean(is_citizen, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_foreign = mean(is_foreign_born, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_other_lang = mean(speaks_other_lang, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Economic
    mean_wage = mean(WAGP, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_wage = median(WAGP, na.rm = TRUE),
    employment_rate = mean(is_employed, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_hours = mean(WKHP, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Education
    pct_bachelor = mean(has_bachelor, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Health
    disability_rate = mean(has_disability, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Transportation
    pct_drive_alone = mean(drives_alone, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_transit = mean(uses_transit, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Housing
    homeownership_rate = mean(is_homeowner, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_vehicles = mean(VEH, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_bedrooms = mean(BDSP, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_value = median(VALP, na.rm = TRUE),

    # Technology
    internet_rate = mean(has_internet, na.rm = TRUE),
    phone_rate = mean(has_phone, na.rm = TRUE),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Remove rows with missing response variables
puma_complete <- puma_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(hicov_rate), !is.na(poverty_rate))

cat("Number of PUMAs with complete data:", nrow(puma_complete), "\n")
cat("\nResponse variable summaries:\n")
cat("Health insurance rate: ",
    sprintf("%.3f (SD=%.3f)", mean(puma_complete$hicov_rate),
            sd(puma_complete$hicov_rate)), "\n")
cat("Poverty rate: ",
    sprintf("%.3f (SD=%.3f)", mean(puma_complete$poverty_rate),
            sd(puma_complete$poverty_rate)), "\n")
```


## Correlation Analysis

```{r correlation}
library(corrplot)

# Correlation matrix for all variables
cor_matrix <- puma_complete %>%
  select(-PUMA, -n) %>%
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Plot correlation matrix
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
         tl.cex = 0.6, tl.col = "black",
         title = "Correlation Matrix (PUMA-level Variables)",
         mar = c(0,0,2,0))

# Find high correlations with each response variable
cat("\n=== Correlations with Response Variables ===\n")

cat("\nTop 10 correlates with Health Insurance Rate:\n")
cor_hicov <- sort(abs(cor_matrix["hicov_rate",]), decreasing = TRUE)
print(head(cor_hicov, 11))  # 11 to include the variable itself

cat("\nTop 10 correlates with Poverty Rate:\n")
cor_poverty <- sort(abs(cor_matrix["poverty_rate",]), decreasing = TRUE)
print(head(cor_poverty, 11))

# Check for multicollinearity among predictors
cat("\n=== High Correlations Among Predictors (|r| > 0.7) ===\n")
predictor_cors <- cor_matrix
predictor_cors[upper.tri(predictor_cors, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
high_cor <- which(abs(predictor_cors) > 0.7, arr.ind = TRUE)

if (nrow(high_cor) > 0) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(high_cor)) {
    var1 <- rownames(cor_matrix)[high_cor[i, 1]]
    var2 <- colnames(cor_matrix)[high_cor[i, 2]]
    cor_val <- cor_matrix[high_cor[i, 1], high_cor[i, 2]]
    cat(sprintf("%s <-> %s: %.3f\n", var1, var2, cor_val))
  }
} else {
  cat("No pairs with |r| > 0.7 found.\n")
}
```

## LASSO for Health Insurance Coverage Rate

```{r lasso_hicov}
# Prepare predictor matrix (exclude response variables and identifiers)
predictors_hicov <- puma_complete %>%
  select(-PUMA, -n, -hicov_rate, -poverty_rate) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Response
y_hicov <- puma_complete$hicov_rate

# Fit LASSO with cross-validation
set.seed(123)
cv_hicov <- cv.glmnet(predictors_hicov, y_hicov, alpha = 1, nfolds = 10)

# Plot CV results
plot(cv_hicov, main = "LASSO CV for Health Insurance Rate")

# Coefficients at lambda.1se (more parsimonious)
coef_hicov_1se <- coef(cv_hicov, s = "lambda.1se")
selected_hicov_1se <- coef_hicov_1se[coef_hicov_1se[,1] != 0,]

# Coefficients at lambda.min (best CV performance)
coef_hicov_min <- coef(cv_hicov, s = "lambda.min")
selected_hicov_min <- coef_hicov_min[coef_hicov_min[,1] != 0,]

cat("\n=== HEALTH INSURANCE COVERAGE RATE ===\n")
cat("\nSelected variables (lambda.1se - more parsimonious):\n")
print(selected_hicov_1se)

cat("\nSelected variables (lambda.min - best CV):\n")
print(selected_hicov_min)

# R-squared on full data
pred_hicov <- predict(cv_hicov, newx = predictors_hicov, s = "lambda.1se")
r2_hicov <- 1 - sum((y_hicov - pred_hicov)^2) / sum((y_hicov - mean(y_hicov))^2)
cat("\nR-squared (lambda.1se):", sprintf("%.3f", r2_hicov), "\n")
```

## LASSO for Poverty Rate

```{r lasso_poverty}
# Prepare predictor matrix
predictors_poverty <- puma_complete %>%
  select(-PUMA, -n, -hicov_rate, -poverty_rate) %>%
  as.matrix()

y_poverty <- puma_complete$poverty_rate

# Fit LASSO with cross-validation
set.seed(123)
cv_poverty <- cv.glmnet(predictors_poverty, y_poverty, alpha = 1, nfolds = 10)

plot(cv_poverty, main = "LASSO CV for Poverty Rate")

# Coefficients
coef_poverty_1se <- coef(cv_poverty, s = "lambda.1se")
selected_poverty_1se <- coef_poverty_1se[coef_poverty_1se[,1] != 0,]

coef_poverty_min <- coef(cv_poverty, s = "lambda.min")
selected_poverty_min <- coef_poverty_min[coef_poverty_min[,1] != 0,]

cat("\n=== POVERTY RATE ===\n")
cat("\nSelected variables (lambda.1se - more parsimonious):\n")
print(selected_poverty_1se)

cat("\nSelected variables (lambda.min - best CV):\n")
print(selected_poverty_min)

# R-squared
pred_poverty <- predict(cv_poverty, newx = predictors_poverty, s = "lambda.1se")
r2_poverty <- 1 - sum((y_poverty - pred_poverty)^2) / sum((y_poverty - mean(y_poverty))^2)
cat("\nR-squared (lambda.1se):", sprintf("%.3f", r2_poverty), "\n")
```

## Summary: Variable Selection Across Both Outcomes

```{r summary}
# Extract non-zero variable names
vars_hicov <- names(selected_hicov_1se)[-1]  # Remove intercept
vars_poverty <- names(selected_poverty_1se)[-1]

# Union of all selected variables
all_selected <- unique(c(vars_hicov, vars_poverty))

cat("\n=== SUMMARY OF VARIABLE SELECTION ===\n")
cat("\nVariables selected for at least one outcome:\n")
print(all_selected)

cat("\n\nVariables selected for BOTH outcomes:\n")
selected_both <- intersect(vars_hicov, vars_poverty)
print(selected_both)

cat("\n\nNumber of variables selected per outcome:\n")
cat("  Health insurance rate:", length(vars_hicov), "\n")
cat("  Poverty rate:", length(vars_poverty), "\n")
```
